workObjectModel.definition: A stable object model such as records, tasks, board items, files, messages, or events that every view and action operates on.
useWhen: Users need repeatable workflows
Multiple components show the same business object
Actions should stay tied to a selected object
avoidWhen: There is no persistent entity or workflow state

multiViewData.definition: The same data exposed through alternate lenses such as table, card, calendar, timeline, board, chart, or detail view.
useWhen: Different roles inspect the same data differently
Users alternate between editing and planning
Data has dates, owners, status, or hierarchy
avoidWhen: The alternate view would hide required editing controls
The data does not have enough structure for the view

contextualActions.definition: Actions placed near the current row, item, selection, panel, or view rather than only in a global menu.
useWhen: Users need to act on selected records
Actions depend on row state or permissions
The page has many possible commands
avoidWhen: The action affects the whole application
The command is rare and destructive without confirmation

sideContextSurfaces.definition: Secondary context shown beside the main surface through drawers, task panes, split panes, side sheets, or panels.
useWhen: Users need reference material while working
The current object should remain visible
The flow is supplemental or reversible
avoidWhen: The secondary content needs the full viewport

semanticStatus.definition: Compact tokens for status, people, files, dates, tags, ownership, priority, and other scan/filter dimensions.
useWhen: Users scan dense lists
Statuses drive filtering or automation
Entities need inline preview or selection
avoidWhen: Color or token text would be the only source of meaning

dashboardComposition.definition: A filterable composition of widgets, summaries, charts, and drill-through links over one or more source datasets.
useWhen: Users monitor portfolio health
Multiple source boards or objects roll up
The primary activity is triage or reporting
avoidWhen: Users need to perform row-level editing as the main task
The source data has unclear ownership or freshness

workflowFeedback.definition: Feedback components that show loading, empty, error, save, progress, notification, and asynchronous operation states.
useWhen: Work can fail, save, queue, refresh, or take time
Users need confidence after actions
The page fetches or mutates data
avoidWhen: Feedback would duplicate native widget state without adding clarity
